name: argo
description: Argo's portable engineering 'way of working' for Claude Code — opinionated but non-breaking. Ships active: on-demand discipline skills, full-lifecycle agent roles (including a product role + write-prd skill that author a lightweight grounded PRD — the durable WHAT/WHY — at the top of the loop, with checkable requirements and a feature→screen matrix that downstream design/code completeness gates consume), a Figma-to-code design pack shaped as a mechanism (provider-neutral core) plus swappable recipes — one recipe implemented today, shadcn-tailwind — (setup-design/figma-audit/figma-sync/figma-create/figma-wireframe/figma-to-code/design-upgrade; the deterministic design-kit modules, skill-script CLI verbs, and reporters ship in the @argohq/kit package this plugin's hooks dispatch through), with a single-file Figma starter as the design source (duplicated once per project, all variables local, theme = modes on the file's own Semantic collection) plus a generated semantic token manifest LLM sessions author against, and a twelfth tier-0 mechanism rule (D24 gap/padding spacing), and five hook categories — plugin-side safety guardrails (dangerous-git, pipe-to-shell, lockfile-edit, bash-source-write, always on), and kit-dispatched gates via hooks/kit-dispatch.mjs → 'argo-hook' (fail-closed when a project declares @argohq/kit but it is missing; allow-with-warning before a project is argo-initialized, so bootstrap can proceed): self-scoping build gates (red-proof, trust — armed only during gated builds via .argo/build-mode.json, inert otherwise), design-guard gates (record/stop/commit — armed per-app by .claude/argo.json design blocks, independent of build-mode, enforcing tier-0 audit receipts and fresh passing spec-diff receipts for componentsPath commits), write hygiene (format-on-write, test-smell), plus a session-start way-of-working card. Opinionated rules ship inert as templates the init skill installs adapted to the host project's stack. Works in any project, with or without the Argo cockpit. See README.
